subject: Add Fanatec wheel driver (#24)
author: Michael Huang

impact.effect_if_absent: Fanatec racing wheels (ClubSport V2/V25, CSL Elite, CSR Elite, Podium DD1/DD2, CSL DD) would not be recognized or controllable as HID input devices; force feedback, pedal calibration, LED control, and steering range adjustment would be unavailable

userspace_coupling.interface: vendor_id 0x0eb7 device enumeration; sysfs attributes for range, load, rumble, display, tuning, LEDs; input event codes with BTN_WHEEL bit set for pedal recognition
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5006 (Fanatec Wheels vendor ID 0x0eb7 check and device-specific handling); 5049 (set_wheel_range call with cfg.wheel_range parameter)

forward_port.effort: carried with modifications: hrtimer_init() + assignment pattern replaced with hrtimer_setup(); missing-prototypes warnings fixed via static keywords and extern declarations in hid-ftec.h; unused variable cleanup

license_provenance.author: gotzl (upstream project author)
license_provenance.origin_project_status: never merged into mainline Linux; remains out-of-tree

notes: This commit introduces a complete new HID driver for Fanatec racing wheels ported from the gotzl/hid-fanatecff out-of-tree project. The driver has two components: hid-ftec.c (probe, sysfs interfaces for load/rumble/display, LED management via led_classdev) and hid-ftecff.c (force-feedback implementation via hrtimer-based effect scheduler). Critical feature: pedal calibration via sysfs 'load' attribute and steering range control via 'range' attribute are essential for hardware compatibility. The driver is tightly coupled to Main_MiSTer userspace via vendor_id checks and wheel-range configuration. Verified not present in vanilla Linux 6.18.38 (no hid-ftec.c, hid-ftecff.c, or HID_FTEC configuration option). Carried patch 0012-hid-fanatec.patch already includes forward-port adjustments for 6.18 (hrtimer API changes, -Wmissing-prototypes fixes, unused variable cleanup). Disposition in patch-provenance.md already correctly classified as Class D carry. Author is PR contributor (#24, #25 Michael Huang, PR #24 also includes fix commit #25 folded in). No upstream equivalence exists.
